title: Talk like a local
setting: You're chatting casually with a friend about a film. It's a chance to use the little words that make you sound native.

steps.speaker: Fründ
steps.es: Häsch de Film gseh? De isch doch mega gsii, oder?
steps.en: Did you see the film? It was amazing, wasn't it?

options.es: Eba, gäll! Ich han nöd dänkt, dass er so guet isch.
options.en: Exactly, right?! I didn't think it'd be so good.
options.feedback: 'Eba, gäll' lands perfectly — you sound completely natural.

options.es: Ja, er isch sehr gut gewesen.
options.en: Yes, it was very good. (High German)
options.feedback: Correct but stiff — the casual particles are what you're practising here.
He's offering energy — match it with a little more to keep the flow.

steps.speaker: Fründ
steps.es: S'Änd het mi aber chli enttüscht, muess ich säge.
steps.en: The ending disappointed me a bit, I have to say.

options.es: Mh, s'Änd isch halt es bitzli schnäll cho, scho.
options.en: Mm, the ending came a bit fast, admittedly.
options.feedback: 'halt', 'es bitzli', 'scho' all in one breath — flawlessly local.
Natural disagreement, easy and warm. Just right.
A bit blunt for a chat about taste — soften it and it flows better.

steps.speaker: Fründ
steps.es: Gömmer s'nöchschte Mal zäme is Kino?
steps.en: Shall we go to the cinema together next time?

options.es: Klar, mach mer! Säg eifach, wänn.
options.en: Sure, let's do it! Just say when.
options.feedback: Easy and keen — 'mach mer' is exactly how a local says yes.
'äuä' is great usage, but it cools the plan — say yes if you mean yes.
Fair, but it shuts a friendly door — a softer no would land warmer.

steps.speaker: Fründ
steps.es: Top. Du redsch übrigens immer meh wie eine vo da, gäll.
steps.en: Great. By the way, you talk more and more like a local, you know.

options.es: Häät, merci! Das ghör ich gärn.
options.en: Aw, thanks! I love hearing that.
options.feedback: Warm and natural, particle and all. You've arrived.
Self-aware and easy — the 'gäll' caps it off beautifully.
Take the compliment — he wouldn't say it if he didn't mean it.
